content.claim: System-level OECD indicators show Hungary has high between-school variance (third-highest among OECD countries), below-average within-school variance, 28.1% of students below proficiency Level 2 (vs. OECD average 23.0%), and socioeconomic background impact of 23.1% (vs. OECD average 14.8%), consistent with a tracked, academically sorted school architecture.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Demography lens
limitations: Between-school variance results from multiple factors (tracking, residential segregation, private/public mix, resource allocation) and cannot be attributed solely to selective-track system
Correlation between variance indicators and outcomes does not prove causal direction or relative policy impact
OECD data are cross-sectional; longitudinal tracking of cohort trajectories not available
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
